reasoning:

We need to estimate if it will be above this on Nov 2, 2025, Nov 25, 2025, and Jan 24, 2026. The statistical model baseline estimates the probability of exceeding the exact price as ~0.47 based strictly on linear trends, but with a 0.57 composite estimate leaning on the recent 5-day positive momentum and favorable YoY seasonality. Next, I investigated ex-dividend dates since dividends structurally lower share prices by the dividend amount ($1.05 per quarter). The Q3 dividend ex-date was Sept 30, paying out Oct 17. Following this pattern, the Q4 dividend ex-date would be in mid-to-late December (likely around Dec 15-20, as the 2024 Q4 ex-div date was mid-Dec). This implies the Nov 2 and Nov 25 resolution dates will NOT cross an ex-dividend date, so they retain a slightly higher probability from pure baseline volatility and recent momentum. However, the Jan 24 resolution date WILL likely cross the Q4 ex-div date in December, creating a ~$1.05 headwind against exceeding the Oct 26 baseline price. 
Furthermore, uncertainty inherently increases over time. For Nov 2 (1 week out), there is decent momentum carrying it forward. For Nov 25 (1 month out), it is closer to 0.5. For Jan 24 (3 months out), predicting real estate asset shifts and crossing a dividend hurdle shifts the odds closer to a coin toss, or slightly lower given the dividend drop, though general market upward drift offsets this. So I predict probabilities of 0.54, 0.52, and 0.51 respectively.
